response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a capable Staff Software Engineer with 15+ years of hands-on development experience across distributed systems and polyglot stacks, but the role demands a C-suite executive with demonstrated team-scaling leadership, production multi-agent AI systems, and deep fintech infrastructure ownership. There is zero evidence of people management, organizational scaling, board-level communication, or operating regulated financial systems at executive seniority. The AI-agent infrastructure work noted in the current role (April 2025, four weeks old at time of evaluation) is too nascent and too junior in framing to satisfy the hard requirement for production multi-agent AI leadership.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background spanning microservices, event-driven architectures, and high-throughput systems with real production scale (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Early-stage exposure to agentic AI workflows and MCP tooling in current role, indicating awareness of the space the JD targets
Entrepreneurial track record via GISCollective with event-driven design and open-source community delivery, showing independent initiative beyond employer scope
concerns: No engineering leadership at any level — no direct reports, no team scaling from 10 to 30+, no governance of outsourced delivery partners, and no organizational design history

Seniority gap is disqualifying: the role requires a C-suite executive with 5+ years of senior technical leadership; the candidate presents and titles as an individual contributor throughout the entire career

No production experience with multi-agent AI in regulated or high-stakes commercial environments, and no fintech-infrastructure depth (credit systems, payment flows, real-time risk) beyond one developer-level role at an investment platform

key_factors.factor: No production multi-agent AI at scale
